1. *Highlight* your confidence by maintaining steady eye contact with the host and panel, even when the questions are challenging. This shows you're prepared and self-assured.

2. *Highlight* your energy by practicing your responses in front of a mirror or recording yourself. This helps you gauge your tone, pacing, and body language, ensuring you come across as lively and engaging.

3. Stay adaptable and ready to think on your feet—game shows often require quick thinking and a sense of humor. Being flexible and open to unexpected twists can make you stand out.

**A man a plan a canal Panama** — this palindrome reminds us that preparation and adaptability are key to success in any performance. 

Remember, being genuine and having fun with the process will always shine through.
